case.reference_id: Unique ID of the human reference analysis used as input.
case.reanalyst_id: ID of the human reanalyst whose path was followed.
case.focal_claim: The focal claim tested in this execution.

execution_overview.overall_summary: Short summary of what the agent executed and what result it obtained.

task_outputs.task_role: Role of this task, e.g., conclusion_oriented_reanalysis or comparable_result_oriented_reanalysis.

executed_analysis.path_name: Name of the executed analysis path.
executed_analysis.software: Software/language actually used, e.g., R, Stata, Matlab, Python.
executed_analysis.model_family: Model or test actually executed, e.g., linear_regression, correlation, t_test.
executed_analysis.run_command: Command actually used to run the analysis, if available.

deviations.deviation_type: dependency_fix, file_path_fix, code_repair, output_extraction_fix, analysis_change, or other.
deviations.description: What changed from the human reference path.
deviations.likely_impact: none, low, medium, high, or unknown.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Short judgment of whether the agent followed the intended human reference analysis.

result_raw.metric: Raw result metric, e.g., coefficient, odds_ratio, correlation, mean_difference, t_statistic, f_statistic.
result_raw.value: Main raw numeric result value, or null if missing.
result_raw.direction: positive, negative, zero, mixed, or unclear.

test_statistics.sample_size: sample size used in the analysis, if available.

result_raw.result_text: Short statement of the raw result.

result_standardized.result_value_std: Converted standardized value, or null if not converted.
result_standardized.conversion_note: Brief explanation of how the standardized result was obtained, or why it could not be converted.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Short conclusion about whether the executed result supports the focal claim.

output_files: Generated result files, tables, logs, figures, or summaries.

failure.failure_reason: Reason for failure, or null if successful.
failure.failure_stage: setup, data_loading, code_execution, result_extraction, standardization, conclusion, or null.

overall_result.overall_conclusion_class: support, inconclusive, opposite, mixed, or unavailable.
overall_result.robustness_basis: Brief explanation of why this case is judged robust or not, based on the executed Task1 and Task2 results.
overall_result.overall_result_summary: Short summary across Task1 and Task2.
overall_result.ready_for_reference_comparison: true if the output contains enough information to compare with the expert reference; false otherwise.
